chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Crl. Bail Application No. 1554 of 2024 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGES For hearing of bail application. 23-09-2024 Mr. Iqbal Hussain Baloch, Advocate for applicant. Ms. Rahat Ahsan, Additional Prosecutor General Sindh Complainant Mudassir Aman, present in person SIP Muhammad Yousuf of P.S. Malir City ============= Omar Sial, J: Mst. Sughra Wife of Muzamilullah has sought post arrest bail in crime number 357 of 2022 registered under sections 324/336- B P.P.C. at the Malir City police station. Her bail application filed before the learned 3rd Additional Sessions Judge, Model Criminal Trial Court, Malir Karachi was dismissed on 03.04.2024. 2. The aforementioned F.I.R. was registered on 04.09.2022 on the complaint of one Mudassir Aman. Mudassir reported that there was a dispute over use of mobile phone between her mother Khair-un-Nisa and sister in law Sughra. On 30.05.2022 while he was on his duty in City Court Malir, he received call whereby his brother Asadullah informed that his mother has burnt. On such information he rushed to his house and shifted her mother to Civil Hospital and she was later on shifted to Patel Hospital for treatment. On regaining her senses, Khair- 2 un-Nisa informed the complainant that on 30.05.2022 she and Sughra were present in the house, she was taking rest on bad when Sughra thrown some liquid on her face as a consequence her face burnt. On her hue and cry her brother reached there and rescued her. 3. I have heard the learned counsel for the Applicant and learned Additional Prosecutor General for the State. My findings are as follows: 4.

I have heard the learned counsel for the Applicant and learned Additional Prosecutor General for the State. My findings are as follows: 4. It appears that earlier bail application No. 1935 of 2022 was disposed of with direction to the trial Court to record the statement of the victim within 30 days. However, the impugned order reflects that the prosecution witnesses remained in attendance but the trial could not proceed owing to absence of the defense counsel as such the bail application filed on the statutory ground was dismissed. Today, learned counsel has not been able to raise even one ground to justify for grant of bail. Bail Application is dismissed. JUDGE
